### Post by Princegiri on 2007-12-30
hai everyone. firstly, im dumping the contents of my fstab below
```
# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config -- 
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/sdc3 :
UUID=fa10b8d7-a8e9-47b6-8147-f63c438436df / ext3 defaults,errors=remount-ro 0 1
# Entry for /dev/sdc4 :
UUID=bc20da7b-89ae-4196-8660-4c812b327a49 /home ext3 defaults 0 2
# Entry for /dev/sda1 :
UUID=420083DB0083D477 /media/sda1 ntfs umask=222,utf8 0 1
# Entry for /dev/sda2 :
UUID=2C88743C8874071C /media/sda2 ntfs umask=222,utf8 0 1
# Entry for /dev/sdb1 :
UUID=9890D5E590D5C9C0 /media/sdb1 ntfs umask=222,utf8 0 1
# Entry for /dev/sdc2 :
UUID=1ed818dd-8b06-483f-a51e-f8409af01011 none swap sw 0 0
/dev/hda /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0
```

what is the "utf8" in the entries for each partitions?? [i have three devices sda, sdb and sdc... sdc is an external usb drive also containing my ubuntu]

the reason for posting this is that, somtimes during bootup, at the splash screen, the monitor goes into FSCK saying something like 
```
dev/sdc3 has been mounted 21 times without check.... checking forced
```
 and does a disk check of the root (and sometimes home)

and the end of this the following message always occurs.... 
[B]
"failed to parse options"
"utf8 is no longer supported" 
"make sure that utf8 is built seperately as a module or built into the kernel"
[/B]
note: these are not the EXACT messages but only the parts i could write down....


my hard disks have been behaving wierdly ever since the error messages started coming....... [are the two even related?].... plus sometimes on restart my bios doesnt boot properly at all.. it just gets stuck.. dont know why....

---

### Post by iris-n on 2007-12-30
The "check forced" is perfectly normal, the system does this at a defined frequency to ensure the health of the HD.

UTF-8 is a text encoding format, I think windows use it in it's partitions, mine did at least.

So i don't think the two are related, although i'm no expert in NTFS.

Exactly when did the error messages began?

---
